thesis_status_reasoning: The system is executing precisely as designed under extreme asymmetric load. Primary pump station sacrificed itself to prevent cavitation damage (acoustic sensor tripped offline), surge relief valve opened to prevent water hammer shock, and gravity reserves deployed immediately while maintaining 97.5% capacity. Sector 4 pressure at 26.5 PSI remains above the critical 20 PSI contamination threshold, demonstrating that passive reserves are successfully sustaining baseline viability during primary component failure.
action_reasoning: All automated failsafes performed correctly, no falsification criteria are met, and gravity reserves have ample capacity (97.5%) to sustain the 24,000 GPM demand while primary systems recover. Sector pressure remains in safe operating range.

key_signals.signal: Pump station load at 0% with cavitation sensor tripped offline - automated protection engaged successfully
Sector 4 pressure at 26.5 PSI - above critical 20 PSI threshold with 6.5 PSI safety margin
Gravity reserves at 97.5% while delivering 24,000 GPM - demonstrates massive reserve capacity
Surge relief valve open - preventing water hammer from sudden pump shutdown
System flow at 24,000 GPM exceeds typical thresholds but gravity reserves stable

biggest_uncertainty: Duration of the extreme 24,000 GPM demand and timeline for primary pump station restoration, which will determine gravity reserve depletion rate
